project: Teyung Tattoo Ink — scroll-driven hero site (web/)
summary: Mobile-only review. The desktop experience passes; mobile does not. The scrub freezes/janks because all frames are held decoded and re-decode on the main thread mid-scroll under memory pressure, and the hero text + header are not laid out for small screens.

issues.title: Scroll jank / freeze — frame sticks then snaps during scrub
issues.symptom: On mobile the frame scrub is not buttery: it stutters, drops frames, and freezes — the frame sticks in place while the page keeps scrolling, then snaps to catch up.

root_cause.name: Main-thread image re-decoding under memory pressure
root_cause.explanation: 240 frames at 1280x720 are preloaded with new Image() and held forever (~880 MB decoded). When mobile memory gets tight the browser evicts the decoded bitmaps but keeps the objects. The rAF loop then calls drawImage() on an evicted frame, forcing a synchronous 1280x720 re-decode on the main thread mid-scroll. That decode blocks the frame (the freeze); scroll runs on the compositor and keeps moving, so the canvas falls behind and snaps when the decode finishes.

contributing_factors.detail: The canvas is redrawn every frame even when currentFrame has settled, continuously competing with the scroll compositor for the main thread.
At mobile DPR 3 the canvas is ~1170x2532 and is fully re-filled every frame with default high-quality smoothing — ~2.25x the pixels of DPR 2 for no visible gain.

contributing_factors.factor: Decoding happens on the main thread
contributing_factors.detail: new Image() (no createImageBitmap / img.decode) means every initial and re-decode lands on the thread that must keep scroll smooth.
All frames request and decode at once, causing a decode storm + network contention that makes the first scroll especially janky.

ordered_solutions.fix: Decode with createImageBitmap() instead of new Image()
ordered_solutions.why: Decodes off the main thread into a GPU-ready bitmap that drawImage will not re-decode. Directly removes the freeze.

ordered_solutions.fix: Stop holding all 240 frames decoded — use a sliding window (decode ~20 around current index, close() the rest) and/or ship a downscaled mobile set (854x480 and/or every 2nd frame -> 120 frames)
ordered_solutions.why: Removes the memory pressure that causes bitmap eviction in the first place.

ordered_solutions.fix: Cap DPR at 2 (Math.min(dpr, 2)) and set ctx.imageSmoothingQuality = 'low' on mobile
ordered_solutions.why: Much cheaper per-frame canvas fill with no visible quality loss.

ordered_solutions.fix: Idle the rAF loop — stop drawing once currentFrame settles, resume on the next scroll change
ordered_solutions.why: Frees the main thread for the compositor between frames so the scrub feels buttery, not just unfrozen.

ordered_solutions.fix: Throttle initial loading (batch ~8-12 frames at a time)
ordered_solutions.why: Avoids the load-time decode storm so the very first scroll is smooth.

issues.expected_impact: Solutions 1 + 2 eliminate the freeze; adding 3 + 4 takes it from 'fixed' to buttery/60fps on mobile.
issues.verification: Test on a real mid-tier Android phone (not just desktop devtools throttling). Watch for: no frame stick/snap during a fast fling scroll, and steady 60fps in the performance profiler with no long main-thread decode tasks.

issues.title: Risk of tab crash from decoded frame memory
issues.symptom: On lower-memory phones the tab can reload or crash outright instead of just janking.

root_cause.name: All 240 frames held decoded in memory
root_cause.explanation: Every frame is preloaded and kept decoded for the page lifetime. 1280x720 decodes to ~3.7 MB each, so 240 frames = ~880 MB resident bitmap. On-disk size (7.7 MB WebP) is misleading — the decoded footprint is what counts. Mobile browsers OOM-crash the tab around 300-400 MB.

ordered_solutions.fix: Sliding-window decode — keep only ~20 frames decoded around the current index and release (close) the rest
ordered_solutions.why: Caps resident memory to a small constant regardless of frame count.

ordered_solutions.fix: Ship a downscaled mobile frame set (e.g. 854x480) and/or halve the frame count to 120
ordered_solutions.why: Cuts per-frame and total resident memory ~4x.

issues.expected_impact: Resident memory drops from ~880 MB to well under the mobile crash threshold; same fix that underpins MOBILE-001.
issues.note: MOBILE-001 (jank) and MOBILE-002 (crash) share the same root memory problem — solving the memory ceiling fixes both.

issues.title: Hero text and header not laid out for small screens
issues.symptom: On a phone the hero copy is cramped and the header is overcrowded.

root_cause.name: Desktop-only layout with no mobile breakpoint
root_cause.explanation: Hero copy sits in a min(680px, 48vw) column with side gradients — cramped on a ~390px phone. The header renders two nav groups plus a clamp(68px,7.5vw,112px) logo with no hamburger/collapse, so navigation crowds the top bar.

ordered_solutions.fix: Collapse the header nav to a hamburger menu and shrink/reposition the logo below a mobile breakpoint
ordered_solutions.why: Removes the crowded top bar and gives the hero room to breathe.

ordered_solutions.fix: Switch hero text to a full-width single-column layout with a bottom-anchored scrim on mobile
ordered_solutions.why: Headline and support text become legible at small widths instead of squeezed into 48vw.

issues.expected_impact: Mobile hero reads as intentional and legible rather than a shrunken desktop layout.
